evals.name: vague-input-needs-clarification
evals.prompt:
I want to use the meta-prompt skill to optimize a task description.

Raw request: "help me write some marketing copy".

Important test note: if the skill calls AskUserQuestion during clarification, assume I answer as follows (so you can complete the full workflow and produce the final prompt):
- Copy type: Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book) outfit-recommendation post
- Audience: urban female office workers aged 20–30
- Length: within 300 Chinese characters
- Style: conversational but tasteful; avoid overt sales voice
- Input material: no specific product; let the target AI invent one as an example
- Target AI: Claude (default)

Follow the meta-prompt skill's 5-step workflow and produce the final prompt (with technique notes). Do NOT execute the produced prompt to write the actual copy.
evals.expected_output: A Claude-XML-formatted prompt text + technique notes, **without** actual marketing-copy output. Should use role (creator viewpoint), output_format (sub-300-char Xiaohongshu post structure), negative constraints (no salesy tone); may use few-shot.

evals.name: clear-input-target-gpt-markdown
evals.prompt:
I want to use the meta-prompt skill to optimize this task:

Raw request: "Write me a prompt I'll send to GPT-4. The task: refactor a Python snippet I'll paste later into object-oriented TypeScript, with these requirements: (1) preserve all original comments and translate them into Chinese, (2) use strict mode, (3) use class style rather than interface+function composition, (4) output a single file."

Many dimensions are already nailed down. Let the skill decide whether further clarification is needed; if it asks, assume I answer: no additional unit tests needed, no JSDoc comment format needed.

Run the full 5-step workflow and produce the final prompt + technique notes. **Do NOT** actually write the TypeScript code.
evals.expected_output: A **Markdown-formatted** prompt text (since target is GPT) + technique notes. Should use ## section headings, delimiter isolation (because the user will paste code), output_format (single file + strict mode + class style), negative constraints (no interface+function, no JSDoc). Should NOT contain XML tags.

evals.name: fact-heavy-cov-heavy-techniques
evals.prompt:
I want to use the meta-prompt skill to optimize this task:

Raw request: "Write me a prompt that asks an AI to compile the history of Federal Reserve rate hikes over the past 5 years and the market reaction. Must be reliable — no making things up."

Test note: if the skill asks clarifying questions, assume I answer:
- Audience: internal investment-research team reference
- Output format: one table listing each hike (date / size / backdrop) + a paragraph per hike analyzing the reaction in US equities and Treasuries
- Target AI: Claude
- Time range: 2020 to present (past 5 years)

Run the full 5-step workflow and produce the final prompt + technique notes. **Do NOT** actually compile the Fed data.
evals.expected_output: A Claude-XML-formatted prompt text + technique notes. Should clearly apply Chain-of-Verification (because the user said 'no making things up'), role (senior macro analyst), output_format (table + paragraphs), negative constraints (no fabrication, no uncited numbers). The technique notes should explain why CoV was chosen.
